zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Alison Bertram於1500年出生在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋時，她的父親George Bertram26歲，母親Alice Francis35歲。她於1521年與James Lawson Esquire在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1547年5月14日在英國英格蘭越可爾維斯特蓋特享年47歲，埋葬在英國諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋聖 尼古拉斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Alison Bertram于1500年出生在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋时，她的父亲George Bertram26岁，母亲Alice Francis35岁。她于1521年与James Lawson Esquire在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1547年5月14日在英国英格兰越可爾維斯特蓋特享年47岁，埋葬在英国諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋聖 尼古拉斯。

nameMeanings.name: Bertram
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English (mainly in southeastern Scotland, of Norman origin), French, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bertram, composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. The raven was the bird of Odin, king of the gods, in ancient Germanic mythology. The personal name was common in France throughout the Middle Ages (especially in the form Bertrand ), where its popularity was increased by the fame of the troubadour Bertrand de Born (c. 1140–1214). The name was taken to England by the Normans in the forms Bertran(d), Bertram, and Bartram.

variants.name: Beltrami
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Dino, Marco, Pio, Sal, Tullio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Beltrame, from an ancient Germanic name composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Bartram
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: variant of   Bertram  .

History: William Bartram, a Quaker, had a large farm near Darby, PA, when his eldest son, John, the first American botanist, was born in 1699. John conducted botanical experiments at his own farm in Kingsessing, PA, near Philadelphia.

variants.name: Bertman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Frieda, Wilhelm.
Americanized form of German Bertmann: altered form (by folk etymology) of Bertram .

variants.name: Bethke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Eldor, Ewald, Otto, Rudi, Wilhelmina.
North German: from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal names   Bertram   or   Berthold  . See also   Beth  .

variants.name: Bertrand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Monique, Francois, Gilles, Raoul, Emile, Herve, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Odette.

French, Walloon, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Bertrand, a variant of Bertram , coined by folk etymology under the influence of the present participle endings -and, -ant.
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Beltran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Luis, Pedro, Jorge, Mario, Ricardo, Francisco, Rafael.
Spanish (Beltrán), Catalan, and southern French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Beltramo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo.
Italian: from the medieval personal name Beltramo, of ancient Germanic origin (see Bertram ).

variants.name: Bert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

variants.name: Battermann
meanings: German:
possibly an occupational name for a merchant or peddler, from Bartermann.
alternatively, perhaps from a short form of the personal name Bertram + Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Bertran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jorge, Eduardo, Enrique, Joaquin, Ricardo, Aleida, Ana, Ana Maria, Elena, Felipe, Jeronimo.  French Andre.
French, Occitan, and Catalan: variant of   Bertram   or   Bertrand  .

nameMeanings.name: Alison
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a very common medieval name, a Norman French diminutive of   Alice .  Despite its early popularity, it became quite rare in England in the 16th century. However, it survived in Scotland, with the result that until its revival in England in the 20th century it had a strongly Scottish flavour.

variants.name: Alyson
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alison .

variants.name: Allyson
meanings: Respelling of   Alison .

variants.name: Allison
meanings: Respelling of   Alison .  This is the more common form of the name in the United States.

variants.name: Alysoun
meanings: Medieval spelling of   Alison  , which has enjoyed some currency since the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Alison Bertram was born in 1500, in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, her father, George Bertram, was 26 and her mother, Alice Francis, was 35. She married James Lawson Esquire in 1521, in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 14 May 1547, in Westgate, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 47, and was buried in St Nicholas, Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Alison Bertram est née en 1500, à Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, George Bertram, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Alice Francis, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé James Lawson Esquire en 1521, à Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 14 mai 1547, à Westgate, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterrée à St Nicholas, Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Alison Bertramが1500年にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドニューカストル ウプオン トインで生まれた時，父George Bertramは26歳，母Alice Francisは35歳であった。1521年にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドニューカストル ウプオン トインでJames Lawson Esquireと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1547年5月14日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアウエストガトで47歳で死去し，イギリスノースウンバーランドニューカストル ウプオン トインスト ニチオラスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Alison Bertram(이)가 1500년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 뉴카스틀 업 타이니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 George Bertram(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Francis(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1521년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 뉴카스틀 업 타이니에서 James Lawson Esquire(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1547년 5월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 웨스트가트에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르스벌랜드 뉴카스틀 업 타이니 스트 닉홀아스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Alison Bertram nasceu em 1500, em Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, George Bertram, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Alice Francis, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com James Lawson Esquire em 1521, em Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 14 de maio de 1547, em Westgate, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 47 anos, e foi sepultada em St Nicholas, Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Inglaterra.

ru.St Nicholas, Newcastle upon Tyne, Northu: ст ничолас, нюкастл упон тин, нортумбирланд, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Alison Bertram родилась в 1500 г. в местности нюкастл упон тин, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени George Bertram было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Francis, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James Lawson Esquire в 1521 г. в местности нюкастл упон тин, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 14 мая 1547 г. в местности вестгэит, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 47 лет и была похоронена в местности ст ничолас, нюкастл упон тин, нортумбирланд, Англия.

es.St Nicholas, Newcastle upon Tyne, Northu: St Nicholas, Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Inglaterra
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Alison Bertram nació en 1500, en Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, George Bertram, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Alice Francis, tenía 35 años. Se casó con James Lawson Esquire en 1521, en Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 14 de mayo de 1547, en Westgate, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 47 años, y fue sepultada en St Nicholas, Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Inglaterra.
